title: ATOME, Active Energy Group, Buccaneer Energy, Empire Metals, Rome Resources
content:
Atome PLC (AIM:ATOM) has landed $420 million in debt financing for its Villeta green fertiliser project in Paraguay. Major backers include the European Investment Bank, the IFC and the Green Climate Fund. CEO Olivier Mussat highlighted the significance of the project due to growing volatility in global fertiliser supply chains.

Active Energy Group (AIM:AEG, OTCID:AEUSF) is acquiring a second grid connection asset, taking its total secured capacity to 13 MVA. The company is targeting 100 megawatts within 18 months, with some sites potentially generating revenue within a month.

Buccaneer Energy (AIM:BUCE, FRA:LMU1) has picked up the Carlisle-1 well, lifting production to around 155 barrels a day. CEO Paul Welch said the proposed Fouke water flood project could play a key role in unlocking further reserves within the Pine Mills field.

Empire Metals (AIM:EEE, OTCQX:EPMLF) reported strong drilling results at its Pitfield titanium project in Australia. Managing director Shaun Bunn emphasised the significance of this discovery for the project’s economics.

Rome Resources (AIM:RMR, FRA:33R) keeps hitting consistent high-grade tin at its Kalayi prospect in the DRC. Intercepts are getting thicker and mineralisation is extending at depth, pointing to a bigger resource ahead as the company moves closer to development.

title: Empire Metals, Atlantic Lithium, ImmuPharma, IP Group, Light Science Technologies, ATOME
content:
Empire Metals Ltd (AIM:EEE, OTCQX:EPMLF) says new drilling confirms a high-grade zone at its Pitfield titanium project in Australia. A bigger drill campaign is underway, with an updated resource expected in the third quarter.

Atlantic Lithium Ltd (AIM:ALL, ASX:A11, OTCID:ALLIF) has secured around $16 million to push its Ewoyaa project in Ghana forward. The funding brings in local pension funds and supports key milestones toward production.

ImmuPharma PLC (AIM:IMM, FRA:25I) is raising up to £7.5 million to develop a new Type 1 diabetes treatment. It also says a licensing deal for its lead drug remains on track this year.

IP Group PLC (LSE:IPO) returned to growth last year with NAV per share up 13% after a tough 2024. The boost came from Pfizer’s Metsera deal and ongoing portfolio exits.

Light Science Technologies Holdings PLC (AIM:LST, FRA:9FD) raised £600,000 in a retail offer that was heavily oversubscribed. Total fundraising now stands at £6.6 million.

ATOME PLC (AIM:ATOM) has earned an “Excellent” rating for its green finance framework. That supports its low-carbon Villeta fertiliser project in Paraguay backed by a $420 million debt deal.
